name: Gefullt Krautroladen (Stuffed Cabbage Rolls)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, cover brown rice with hot water and soak for 3 hours.
Drain thoroughly.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan, combine the rice, 3 cups water, and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt.
Simmer covered for 40 minutes or until liquid is absorbed. Stir in dill seeds,
marjoram, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper.
3. In a large skillet, saute chopped onion (1 1/2 cups) in hot vegetable oil
(5 tbsp) until soft, about 6 to 8 minutes. Add paprika and minced garlic,
continue cooking for 2 minutes.
4. Stir in the rice mixture, eggs, bread crumbs, and parsley.
Adjust seasonings to taste.
5. Core cabbage and blanch the cored-side-down in boiling salted water
(5 minutes or until softened). Drain.
6. Remove 12 leaves and cut off one fourth of each leaf from the base.
Arrange one leaf curved-side down on a square of dampened cheesecloth.
Place 3 cups of rice mixture in the center. Wrap the leaf around the filling
and twist the corners of the cheesecloth to form the roll. Repeat with remaining
leaves.
7. Chop remaining cabbage and saute with chopped onions (1 cup) and vegetable oil
(5 tbsp) until soft. Add tomatoes, vermouth, beef broth, tomato paste,
sugar, salt, pepper. Simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Adjust seasonings.
8. Transfer cabbage-tomato sauce mixture to a large baking dish.
Arrange the cabbage rolls close together in one layer on top of the sauce.
Spoon some of the sauce over the rolls. Bake at 325°F for 1 1/2 hours,
basting every 4-5 times during cooking.
9. Let the dish cool, then cover and refrigerate overnight.
Remove cheesecloth before serving. Reheat in a preheated oven
(350°F) for 30 minutes before serving.
